nav.tooltipDocs: The .md files in your vault — add `kind:` to frontmatter and they become ontology nodes
nav.tooltipOntology: Hierarchical view of frontmatter nodes (project → domain → capability → element)
nav.tooltipTopology: Project dependency map — one of the ontology's exit views

modeBadge.vaultTooltip: Vault mode — {name} ({count} docs). Every change is saved to your local disk.
modeBadge.cloudTooltip: Cloud mode — Firestore real-time sync. Changes are saved to the cloud.
modeBadge.demoTooltip: Demo mode — no vault picked, signed out. Build-time demo manifest only; changes are not saved.

landing.subtitle: A codebase ontology that humans and AI agents author together. Markdown frontmatter is the graph — view it as tree, topology, or ERD. Like Obsidian or Notion, just point at a folder and start.
landing.topologyCaption: TOPOLOGY · {nodes, plural, one {# NODE} other {# NODES}} · {relations, plural, one {# RELATION} other {# RELATIONS}}
landing.step1Body: Frontmatter keys alone create nodes and relations. AI agents (via MCP) read and write the same vault.
landing.step2Body: "Who uses this capability?" "What breaks if I change it?" — answers in 0 clicks instead of grepping markdown.
landing.step3Body: The same ontology as tree, topology, and ERD. Domain census, blast radius, dependency chains, all on one screen.
landing.privacyNote: Local folders stay on your disk and are never sent anywhere. Your data, your truth source.
landing.returnTargetSubtitle: The screen you requested needs sign-in. After you sign in we'll take you straight back.

sigma.ariaLabel: Project topology map — jump to individual projects from the hub bar on the left

controls.searchCountAriaLabel: {visible} / {total, plural, one {# node} other {# nodes}} shown

projectKnowledge.heading: Project topology
projectKnowledge.description: When the markdown documents you registered are made public, documents and items are decomposed and connected here as a single project topology.
projectKnowledge.emptyManageHeadline: No knowledge graph for this project yet.
projectKnowledge.emptyManageBody: Register a markdown document and an extraction job is queued automatically; once approved and made public, the items and connections appear here.
projectKnowledge.emptyViewerHeadline: No knowledge graph for this project yet.
projectKnowledge.emptyViewerBody: Once the workspace owner registers a markdown document for this project and makes it public, the items and connections will appear here right away.
projectKnowledge.sceneOpenDetailHint: Click to open the document evidence detail.

tree.evidenceChipTitle: {count} pieces of evidence for this node — click for details
tree.searchPlaceholder: Find a node in the tree — Korean or English

manualNode.title: Add a new node manually
manualNode.description: Create an ontology node directly (the same manual source as vault frontmatter / the builder). Use this to quickly add a single concept.
manualNode.idHelp: Letters / digits / . - _ : only. Auto-suggested from the title — edit as needed.
manualNode.idCollision: This ID already exists as the node "{title}". Pick a different ID.
manualNode.summaryPlaceholder: What this node means in a line or two.
manualNode.notePlaceholder: Why you created this node by hand, future backfill plans, etc.
manualNode.alreadyExists: ID "{id}" already exists (with a different source). Try another ID or look up the existing node.
manualNode.submitErrorFallback: Something went wrong while creating the node.

manualEdge.title: Add a new relation manually
manualEdge.description: Draw a relation between two nodes (from → to). Identical (type, from, to) edges are deduped automatically.
manualEdge.fromMissing: From node not found. Close the modal and pick another node from the tree.
manualEdge.toSearchPlaceholder: Search by node title or ID
manualEdge.edgeCollision: An edge with the same (type, from, to) already exists.
manualEdge.labelPlaceholder: Use when the type alone isn't enough.
manualEdge.notePlaceholder: Why you created this relation by hand.
manualEdge.submitErrorFallback: Something went wrong while creating the relation.

globalSearch.dialogDescription: Search concepts (ontology nodes), documents, and projects in one place. Use up/down arrows to move, Enter to select, ESC to close.
globalSearch.placeholderWithProjects: Search concepts and projects — KR/EN mix OK
globalSearch.emptyNoCorpus: Nothing indexed yet. Create your first node in vault frontmatter or the builder and it will appear here.

projectSearch.helpRecent: Showing recently updated projects first. Narrow down by name, tags, or stack.
projectSearch.helpKeyboard: Use up/down arrows to move through results and Enter to select. ESC to close.
projectSearch.emptyNoProjectsTitle: No projects in this space yet.
projectSearch.emptyNoProjectsBody: Once projects are added, you can search and jump straight to them here.
projectSearch.emptyLayerBody: No projects match the selected “{layer}”. Reset the filter to see the full list again.
projectSearch.emptyNoMatchBody: Couldn't find “{query}” in any name, tag, or stack. Try a different word or clear the search to return to the full list.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.subtitle: Navigate the map without a mouse.
shortcuts.help: List of keyboard shortcuts available for navigation, the guided tour, and portfolio mode.

rows.openGlobalPalette: Search ontology, docs, and projects together
rows.toggleDocsDrawer: Toggle the docs vault preview drawer (with edit access)
rows.stepCloseOverlays: Close drawers and overlays one step at a time
rows.doubleClickLocal: Show only neighbors of the selected node (local graph)
rows.shiftClickPath: Highlight the shortest path between two nodes
rows.tabNeighbor: Move to a neighbor of the selected node
rows.queryCommandPrefix: Prefix the query for command mode
rows.queryTagPrefix: Prefix the query for tag mode
rows.scrollHeading: TOC sidebar follows the current heading
rows.hoverNeighbor: Highlight 1-hop neighbors and dim the rest
rows.togglePillView: Switch view via the top pill
rows.localVault: Use a local folder as your vault (File System Access)
rows.focusRefresh: Auto-refresh the local vault on tab focus
rows.pinDoc: Pin / unpin (pinned section stays on top)
rows.copyDocUrl: Copy current document URL to clipboard
rows.tagFilter: Click a tag chip to filter the tree
rows.modeToggle: Mode toggle — show only that mode's docs

hero.summaryDefault: Reading the connection structure that grows from your documents.
hero.docsVaultAriaLabel: Open docs vault — browse local folders and server docs in one place
hero.ontologyAriaLabel: Open ontology tree — view nodes and relations from vault frontmatter as a hierarchy

workspaceStrip.stubAriaLabel: Unresolved references — promote or discard from the tree
workspaceStrip.stubTitle: Nodes referenced via relates.target in frontmatter that don't exist yet. Promote or discard from the stub list at the bottom of /ontology.

gestureHint.body: Two-finger pinch to zoom, drag to pan. Tap a node to open details.

docsDrawer.totalLine: View all {count} registered documents at a glance.
docsDrawer.openAllAriaLabel: Open the full document vault page
docsDrawer.filterPlaceholder: Quick filter by title or path…
docsDrawer.filterAriaLabel: Filter documents by title or path

projectDrawer.openDocsVaultTitleWithDoc: Open the top related document for {name}
projectDrawer.evidenceFallback: Collected clues that describe this project from public documents.
projectDrawer.summaryStandalone: Currently reads as a standalone project.

editor.discardConfirm: Discard unsaved changes and close the editor?

stats.title: Vault stats
stats.tagsEmpty: No documents have frontmatter tags yet.
stats.orphanHint: Disconnected from any other doc. Manual link candidates.

depsBar.notRegistered: This document isn't registered in Folder-Topology — move it to the projects/ directory and fill `name` · `category` in frontmatter to make it appear as a node.
depsBar.circularConfirm:
Warning: {dep} already depends on {current}.
Adding this dependency creates a cycle. Continue?

radar.title: Suggested links
radar.intro: Planners review related candidates here. Reviewed and dismissed states persist to the vault; actual links are added by editing.
radar.emptyText: No candidates to show. Restore dismissed candidates to review them again.
radar.hiddenNote: {count} dismissed candidates are hidden from this document.

empty.selectPrompt: Pick a document from the tree on the left

outline.editTooltip: Edit this file (saves directly to the local vault)
outline.copyTooltip: Copy URL to this document to clipboard
outline.printTooltip: Open browser print dialog (save as PDF)
outline.deleteTooltip: Delete this document from the local vault

page.title: Ontology Builder
page.helpIntro: A workspace where you draw your knowledge graph by hand.
page.helpStepPalette: Click a kind from the palette on the left to add a new node
page.helpStepConnect: Drag from a node's handle and drop on another node to add a relation
page.helpStepEphemeral: Ephemeral nodes are dashed indigo — name them in the inspector and save
page.helpShortcuts: N · new node  /  Del · delete selected  /  Esc · clear selection  /  F · fullscreen
page.exportAriaLabel: Export current canvas as frontmatter md
page.exportJsonLdAriaLabel: Export current canvas as JSON-LD (RDF 1.1 compatible)
page.exportGraphMlAriaLabel: Export current canvas as GraphML (Gephi / Cytoscape)
page.clearAriaLabel: Clear {nodes} ephemeral nodes and {edges} ephemeral relations
page.layoutDagreTitle: Kind hierarchy LR (project → domain → capability → element)
page.autoLayoutTooltip: Reset every node's position to the auto-layout result (frontmatter canvasPosition is preserved — your coordinates return on next visit)
page.mobileTitle: The builder shines on a bigger screen
page.mobileBody: It's a 3-pane ERD layout (palette · canvas · inspector), so the columns overlap on mobile. Open it again on desktop or tablet landscape. Meanwhile you can browse the ontology in tree or topology view.
page.toastNoAccount: No account detected. Please sign in.
page.toastDemoMode: Demo mode — can't save. Open a vault folder under /docs or sign in.
page.confirmDeleteWithBacklinks:
Deleting "{slug}" will leave {count} nodes dangling ({preview}{rest}).

Delete anyway?
page.confirmDelete: Delete "{slug}" from the vault? This cannot be undone.

blastRadius.titleWithBacklinks: {count} nodes depend on this — delete anyway?
blastRadius.bodyClean: The node and its frontmatter will be removed from the vault. This cannot be undone.
blastRadius.bodyWithBacklinks: These nodes reference the one you're about to delete. Their links will become dangling — you can fix them after, but plan a cleanup pass.
blastRadius.hintClean: Tip: builder edits write straight to disk. Re-create the node any time.
blastRadius.hintWithBacklinks: Tip: open the listed nodes after delete to remove the dangling reference, or rename instead of deleting.

canvas.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first node.

inspector.subtitle: Selected node detail and inline edits.
inspector.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first node, or click any canvas node to see its detail here.
inspector.saveButtonAriaLabel: Save this node as a manual node
inspector.ephemeralFooterReady: On save, this is written to a .md file in the vault (or to Firestore in cloud mode).
inspector.vaultFooterReadOnly: Dogfood manifest — baked at build time. Open a vault folder under /docs to edit directly.
inspector.vaultFooterEditable: Only the title is patched into frontmatter `title:`. Body and other keys stay untouched. AI agents (over MCP) see the same vault and reflect changes immediately.
inspector.literalAutoSaveClean: Saving with an empty value removes the frontmatter key entirely.

onboarding.title: More than an ERD — let's draw your domain *map*
onboarding.intro: Not a code or DB schema, but a visual map of *features, relationships, and roles*. PMs, designers, and ops folks can draw it themselves. AI agents (Claude Code, …) add nodes to the same map.
onboarding.stepPaletteStrong: Click a kind on the left
onboarding.stepPaletteBody: — Project · Domain · Capability · Element. A draft node lands in the middle. (Code identifiers: project / domain / capability / element)
onboarding.stepConnectStrong: Drag from a node's edge handle
onboarding.stepConnectBody: and drop on another node — the two get a *relation (depends / contains / describes)* between them.
onboarding.stepSaveBody: — straight to disk as `.md` in vault mode, or to Firestore in cloud mode. AI agents read and write the same map over MCP.
onboarding.shortcutsHint: Quick keys — N new node · Del delete · Esc cancel

palette.subtitle: Pick a kind and click — a new node lands in the middle of the canvas.
palette.footerHint: New nodes are ephemeral — type a name in the inspector on the right and save before they go into vault/.md.
palette.kindProjectHint: Top-level — one project in the whole workspace
palette.kindDomainHint: A big area inside a project — auth / billing / notifications, …
palette.kindCapabilityHint: One feature inside a domain — verb-style like "sign in"
palette.kindElementHint: A small piece inside a capability — screen / button / API

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: Activity and structure stats for the whole ontology. The tree shows hierarchy; insights show where the hubs are and what grew most recently.
insights.emptyTitleBefore: Your ontology is empty. Start from the
insights.projectPanelSubtitle: {count} projects total — domain · capability · element sums, sorted by size
insights.edgeTypePanelSubtitle: {count} relations total — split by semantic relation type
insights.crossProjectPanelSubtitle: Edges where the two nodes belong to different projects — dependency chains
insights.crossProjectFooterAfter: page's strong-relations list — rows with a `cross` chip stand out.
insights.activityPanelSubtitle: {count} nodes updated in the past 30 days
insights.activityEmpty: No nodes were updated in the past 30 days.
insights.orphansPanelSubtitle: {count} nodes not hanging from the tree
insights.orphansEmpty: Every node is connected to the tree.

relations.title: Relations
relations.subtitle: Nodes live in the tree, stats live in insights. Relations show the distribution of *semantic relation types*.
relations.emptyTitleBefore: No approved relations yet. Start by reviewing nodes in the
relations.strongPanelSubtitleBase: Top 12 with the richest evidence — most-cited semantic links
relations.crossChipTitle: These two nodes are in different projects — a cross-project dependency

selector.emptyDescCanCreate: Create your first project to get going.
selector.quickCreateSubmit: Create and write the first doc
selector.newProjectDesc: Quickly add a new project from the bottom of the list and jump to its first doc.
selector.cardDescriptionFallback: This project doesn't have a description yet.

detail.stateInvalidDesc: No valid project slug, so detail can't be displayed.
detail.connectionSummaryStandalone: {name} reads as a standalone project today.
detail.inlineDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line
detail.depCardDescription: Pick the hubs and services this project relies on; the topology updates instantly. Cycle-causing candidates are disabled.
detail.neighborMapDescription: A small map containing only the projects directly linked to this one. Drag, hover, and click behave just like the main topology.
detail.knowledgeTopologyDescription: Shows concepts and connections decomposed from md docs registered to this project as a graph. Unlike the home "Workspace map" (project-to-project dependencies), this scopes to one project's content.
detail.infoCardDescription: Project description written by the workspace owner.
detail.evidenceDescription: Reads the public evidence behind the home map in document → concept → connection-reason order.
detail.ontologyReasonEdges: Documents repeatedly mention {labels}, which is why this project shows up alongside them.
detail.ontologyReasonEmpty: Not enough public documents yet — once more docs accumulate, you'll see why this project is connected here.
detail.neighborsMoreNote: You can browse all {count} connections in the connection map section of the main content.
detail.neighborsEmpty: Not many directly linked projects yet.

editor.confirmDiscardChanges: You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
editor.loadErrorDuplicate: The project to duplicate could not be found.
editor.createdAndOpenToast: "{name}" created · fill in details on the edit screen
editor.headerSubtitle: An edit screen that organizes metadata, connections, screenshots, and detailed descriptions in one place.
editor.openPublicAria: Open the public view in a new tab
editor.savedNotice: Just saved. You can keep refining here, or open the public view to verify the result immediately.
editor.tipTitleEasiest: Fill in name, category, status, and a short description, then save first.
editor.tipDescEasiest: Reopen later to enrich connections, screenshots, and operational info gradually.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep1: 1. The slug auto-fills as you type the name.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep2: 2. A single short description fills out the public card and drawer.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep3: 3. Detail fields can be expanded below and filled in as needed.
editor.publicCompanionDesc: Even while editing, you can open the public detail and linked docs to see the actual published state. Changes appear immediately on the public view after saving and reopening.

header.title: Docs Vault
header.roleTooltip: Current role: {role}. No edit permission — read-only.
header.paletteTooltip: Search · commands · tags — Tab to switch audience

advanced.ontologyHintPrefix: First time? Try selecting this repo's
advanced.ontologyHintSuffix: — this tool's own ontology (21 nodes) appears in the tree instantly.

topology.emptyBody: Auto-generate the Folder-Topology file structure (projects/_.md, categories.md, statuses.md) and the topology will show up. Existing files are not overwritten.
topology.scaffoldCta: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

commands.scaffoldTopology: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

dialog.deleteConfirm:
Really delete?

{title}
{slug}.md

This cannot be undone.
dialog.scaffoldConfirm:
Initialize this folder as a Folder-Topology vault?

- projects/ directory + 2 sample projects
- categories.md · statuses.md default sets
- README.md

Existing files are not overwritten.
dialog.scaffoldDone:
Initialization complete — {created} new files · {skipped} skipped

For field specs, open the freshly generated README.md first. Use the 2 sample projects (sample-hub / sample-leaf) frontmatter as a template.
dialog.noHeadings: This document has no h2/h3 headings.
dialog.notLocalFile: This document is not a local vault file.
dialog.notRendered: Document is not rendered yet. Try again.
dialog.importOverwriteConfirm:
{count} documents already exist in the vault.

Yes = Overwrite (saveDoc)
No = Skip duplicates

Proceed?

topology.selectionAnnouncement: {name} selected. {deps, plural, one {# dependency} other {# dependencies}}, {referenced, plural, one {# reference} other {# references}}.

workspace.subtitle: Workspace · {projects} projects · {hubs} hubs{growth}
workspace.description: {hubs} hubs are at the heart of {projects} projects. Click a node to open its details.

empty.noMatchesBody: No projects match the current filters.
empty.kicker: TOPOLOGY · {count, plural, one {# NODE} other {# NODES}}
empty.bodyNoProjects: Connect a markdown folder or create your first project in the builder. Fill in `dependencies:` in the frontmatter and lines appear here automatically.
empty.bodyNoDeps: Add a single dependency between two projects (`dependencies:` frontmatter) and a line will appear here automatically. The builder canvas can also draw them via drag.
empty.ctaBuilder: Add a dependency in the builder

hint.title: Project terrain map
hint.body: Nodes are projects, lines are dependencies. Click to open details, search to narrow.
